transcript.speaker: doctor
transcript.text: Hello, Priya. This is your doctor speaking.

transcript.speaker: doctor
transcript.text: What brings you to consultation today?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: My throat is paining difficult swallowing.

transcript.speaker: doctor
transcript.text: How long have you been experiencing this for?

transcript.speaker: doctor
transcript.text: Can you describe the severity of your throat pain?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Itchy feeling. Pain in the throat.

transcript.speaker: doctor
transcript.text: Can you rate the severity of your throat pain?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: My throat is paining difficult swallowing.

subjective: Patient reports difficulty swallowing and itchy feeling in the throat
Patient has been experiencing throat pain for 2 days
Patient reports being unable to swallow properly
objective: No objective findings were discussed in this consultation
assessment: Acute pharyngitis or tonsillitis is a possible cause of the patient's symptoms
plan: Further evaluation is needed to determine the cause of the throat pain
Consider ordering a throat swab or other diagnostic tests
Recommend over-the-counter pain relievers that are safe for the patient to take, considering their aspirin and NSAID allergy
Schedule a follow-up appointment to monitor the patient's symptoms and adjust the plan as needed

aiSummary.narrative: The patient presents with a 2-day history of throat pain and difficulty swallowing, with an itchy feeling in the throat. The patient has a history of asthma and allergies to aspirin and NSAIDs. A possible cause of the symptoms is acute pharyngitis or tonsillitis. The plan includes further evaluation and consideration of ordering diagnostic tests, as well as recommending safe pain relievers and scheduling a follow-up appointment.
medications: Consider prescribing a safe pain reliever for the patient's throat pain
warnings: The patient has a history of allergies to aspirin and NSAIDs, which should be considered when prescribing pain relievers
